lines.translation: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes, like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on, but he felt that neither the style nor the color was very good.

Today is Sunday. It just so happens that Lin Xuemei and Lisha also need to buy daily necessities like toilet paper, toothpaste, towels, and laundry detergent, so Ke Lin took them to the biggest shopping center nearby.
What clothes do you want to buy?
I'd like to buy a tracksuit.
They're right over here. Take a look at this set. The style, size, and length are all perfect, and it's 20% off.
The color isn't bad either. How much is it? What's the brand?
The price is not expensive. I haven't heard of this brand.
However, it is pure cotton.
It has to be a good brand. I want to buy a name brand.
You're so fashionable! Wearing name brands! That one looks like a name brand... Oh my, it's too expensive!
When I shop, I only buy name brands, otherwise I don't buy at all, because name-brand clothes are better quality.

That's right. Some clothes are cheap, it's true, but the brand isn't good. After you wear them once or twice, you don't want to wear them anymore, so you have to buy another one. The money you spend on two pieces of clothing might be more than buying one name-brand item.
What you said makes sense.

Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands. My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they're comfortable to wear, second, whether the quality is good, and third, whether the price is suitable. I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not. Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.
I disagree. Do you like to see Xuemei wearing clothes that don't look good?
Xuemei looks good in any clothes, right?
Okay, okay, okay, stop talking.
Hey, Ke Lin, how come the shirt you're wearing is a name brand? Don't you not wear name brands?
I said you don't necessarily have to buy name brands, I never said I don't wear them. I bought this one when it was on sale.
Hey, Lisha, let's go check out the daily necessities section.
You guys go ahead. I'm going to pay first. I'll see you in a bit.
(Zhang Tianming is at the checkout...)
Sir, will you be paying with cash or credit card?
I'll use my card.
Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.
Okay... Thank you! Goodbye.

example_sentence.cn: 买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好,但是也不一定非买名牌的不可。我买衣服的标准,第一是看穿着舒服不舒服,第二是质量好不好,第三是价钱合适不合适。是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。因为穿衣服是为了自己,不是为了给别人看。
example_sentence.py: mǎi yīfu zhǐ kǎolǜ piányi dāngrán bù hǎo dànshì yě bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bùkě wǒ mǎi yīfu de biāozhǔn dìyī shì kàn chuān zhe shūfu bù shūfu dì'èr shì zhìliàng hǎo bù hǎo dìsān shì jiàqian héshì bù héshì shì shénme páizi de yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo wǒ dōu bù zàihu yīnwèi chuān yīfu shì wèile zìjǐ bù shì wèile gěi biérén kàn
example_sentence.translation: Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands. My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they're comfortable to wear, second, whether the quality is good, and third, whether the price is suitable. I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not. Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.

example.cn: 买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好,但是也不一定非买名牌的不可。我买衣服的标准,第一是看穿着舒服不舒服,第二是质量好不好,第三是价钱合适不合适。是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。因为穿衣服是为了自己,不是为了给别人看。
example.py: mǎi yīfu zhǐ kǎolǜ piányi dāngrán bù hǎo dànshì yě bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bùkě wǒ mǎi yīfu de biāozhǔn dìyī shì kàn chuānzhe shūfu bù shūfu dì'èr shì zhìliàng hǎo bù hǎo dìsān shì jiàqian héshì bù héshì shì shénme páizi de yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo wǒ dōu bú zàihu yīnwèi chuān yīfu shì wèile zìjǐ bú shì wèile gěi biérén kàn
example.translation: Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands. My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they are comfortable to wear; second, whether the quality is good; and third, whether the price is suitable. I don't care what brand it is, or whether the style is fashionable or not. Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.

example_sentence.cn: 是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。
example_sentence.py: Shì shénme páizi de, yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo, wǒ dōu bù zàihu.
example_sentence.translation: I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not.

example.cn: 是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。
example.py: Shì shénme páizi de, yàngzi shímáo bu shímáo, wǒ dōu bù zàihu.
example.translation: I don't care what brand it is, or whether the style is fashionable or not.

example_sentence.cn: 我不同意。
example_sentence.py: Wǒ bù tóngyì.
example_sentence.translation: I disagree.

example.cn: 我不同意。
example.py: Wǒ bù tóngyì.
example.translation: I don't agree.

example_sentence.cn: 雪梅穿什么衣服都好看,对不对?
example_sentence.py: Xuěméi chuān shénme yīfu dōu hǎokàn, duì bu duì?
example_sentence.translation: Xuemei looks good in any clothes, right?

example.cn: 雪梅穿什么衣服都好看,对不对？
example.py: Xuěméi chuān shénme yīfu dōu hǎokàn, duì bu duì?
example.translation: Xuemei looks good in whatever she wears, right?

vocabulary.definition: in a moment, a little while

example_sentence.cn: 你们去吧,我先去付钱,我们一会儿见。
example_sentence.py: nǐmen qù ba wǒ xiān qù fùqián wǒmen yíhuìr jiàn
example_sentence.translation: You guys go ahead. I'm going to pay first. I'll see you in a bit.

historical_usages.definition: in a moment, a little while

example.cn: 你们去吧,我先去付钱,我们一会儿见。
example.py: nǐmen qù ba wǒ xiān qù fù qián wǒmen yīhuìr jiàn
example.translation: You guys go ahead. I'll go pay first. We'll meet in a bit.

example_sentence.cn: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡?
example_sentence.py: Xiānsheng, fù xiànjīn, háishi yòng xìnyòngkǎ?
example_sentence.translation: Sir, will you be paying with cash or credit card?

example.cn: 先生,付现金,还是用信用卡？
example.py: Xiānsheng, fù xiànjīn, háishì yòng xìnyòngkǎ?
example.translation: Sir, are you paying with cash or credit card?

example_sentence.cn: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
example_sentence.py: xiān sheng jiā shàng shuì yí gòng shì yì bǎi bā shí liù kuài sì
example_sentence.translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

example.cn: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
example.py: xiānsheng jiāshang shuì yígòng shì yì bǎi bā shí liù kuài sì
example.translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

example_sentence.cn: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
example_sentence.py: Xiānsheng, jiāshang shuì yīgòng shì yībǎi bāshíliù kuài sì.
example_sentence.translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

example.cn: 先生,加上税一共是一百八十六块四。
example.py: Xiānsheng, jiāshang shuì yīgòng shì yībǎi bāshíliù kuài sì.
example.translation: Sir, with tax, the total is one hundred eighty-six dollars and forty cents.

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.explanation_en: Words have entered Mandarin from a number of sources, including English and Chinese dialects. T恤衫 is a portmanteau composed of T恤, the Cantonese transliteration of the English "T-shirt" (pronounced tiseot in Cantonese), and the Mandarin morpheme 衫, “shirt.”

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: 牛仔裤 (lit. cowboy pants) is the word for jeans. The word 仔 is used in several southern dialects to mean “boy.” The word 牛仔, which has since been adopted into Mandarin, originated from Cantonese as a direct translation of the English word "cowboy."

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: 卫生纸 (lit. hygiene paper) is also called 手纸 in northern spoken Chinese.

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: The word 质量, meaning “quality,” is used in Mainland China, whereas the word 品质 is preferred in Taiwan, where it is pronounced pinzhí. In Mainland China, 品质 is pronounced pinzhì and has a slightly different meaning-it refers to the essential qualities of a person or thing, such as a person's "moral fiber,” 道德品质 (dàodé pinzhì).

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 有道理 means “to make sense.” It is often used to concur with someone else's opinion.

grammar_sections.title: 无论…,都…
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 无论, meaning “regardless,” signifies that a result will remain the same under any condition or circumstance. It must be used with either a question pronoun, as in (A) and (B), or an alternative construction, as in (C) and (D).

patterns.pattern: 无论 + Alternative Construction (A not A / A 还是 B), ...都...

examples.hanzi: 明天无论谁请客我都不去。
examples.pinyin: Míngtiān wúlùn shéi qǐngkè wǒ dōu bú qù.
examples.translation: It doesn't matter who's paying tomorrow. I'm not going.

examples.hanzi: 城里的购物中心我都没去过，所以无论去哪个都可以。
examples.pinyin: Chéng lǐ de gòuwù zhōngxīn wǒ dōu méi qùguò, suǒyǐ wúlùn qù nǎge dōu kěyǐ.
examples.translation: I haven't been to any of the shopping centers in town, so going to any of them will be fine.

examples.hanzi: 我们已经说好明天去买东西，你无论愿意不愿意都得跟我们去。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yǐjīng shuō hǎo míngtiān qù mǎi dōngxi, nǐ wúlùn yuànyì bù yuànyì dōu děi gēn wǒmen qù.
examples.translation: We've already decided to go shopping tomorrow. Whether you're willing or not, you have to go with us.

examples.hanzi: 他无论在家里还是在学校，都在学习，很少看见他玩儿。
examples.pinyin: Tā wúlùn zài jiālǐ háishì zài xuéxiào, dōu zài xuéxí, hěn shǎo kànjiàn tā wánr.
examples.translation: Whether he's at home or at school, he's always studying. You rarely see him have fun.

usage_notes: The conjunction 无论 (wúlùn) introduces a clause with two or more alternatives, indicating that the result in the main clause will remain the same regardless of the choice made.
The clause following 无论 (wúlùn) must contain a question pronoun (e.g., 谁, 什么, 哪儿, 怎么) or an alternative construction (e.g., A 不 A, A 还是 B).
The main clause must contain an adverb, usually 都 (dōu), to emphasize the invariable result.

grammar_sections.title: Non-predicative adjectives
grammar_sections.explanation_en:
Some adjectives are primarily used as attributives, that is, before nouns or noun phrases. Unlike other adjectives, they cannot be qualified by words such as 很 or 非常. Their function is to categorize or distinguish the inherent traits or characteristics of people or things. Non-predicative adjectives that we have studied include 男, 女, 国际, 有机, and 纯棉, as in 男朋友 (boyfriend), 女朋友 (girlfriend), 国际足球 (international football), 有机水果 (organic fruit), 有机咖啡 (organic coffee), 纯棉衬衫 (one-hundred-percent cotton shirt), and 纯棉运动服 (one-hundred-percent cotton tracksuit).

When these adjectives are used as predicates, they must be embedded in a 是……的 construction:

patterns.meaning_en: To use a non-predicative adjective as a predicate to categorize or distinguish something.

examples.hanzi: 李友的中国朋友是个男的。
examples.pinyin: Lǐ Yǒu de Zhōngguó péngyou shì ge nán de.
examples.translation: Li You's Chinese friend is male.

examples.hanzi: 小李买的水果都是有机的。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Lǐ mǎi de shuǐguǒ dōu shì yǒujī de.
examples.translation: The fruit Little Li buys is all organic.

examples.hanzi: 我穿的这件衬衫是纯棉的。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ chuān de zhè jiàn chènshān shì chúnmián de.
examples.translation: This shirt I'm wearing is one-hundred-percent cotton.

grammar_sections.title: Adjective/verb + 是 + adjective/verb, 可是 or 但是… revisited
grammar_sections.explanation_en: As you will recall from IC2, this structure is equivalent to “. . . is . . . (but) . . .” [See Grammar 6, Lesson 11, IC2.] It’s used to express partial agreement. The speaker concurs with the other person’s statement before going on to point out a different aspect that the other person may have ignored, as in (A) and (B), or warn the other person of possible contingencies, as in (C).

patterns.pattern: Adjective/Verb + 是 + Adjective/Verb, 可是/但是…

examples.hanzi:
A: 我打算学音乐。
B: 学音乐好是好, 可是以后找工作不太容易。
examples.pinyin:
A: Wǒ dǎsuàn xué yīnyuè.
B: Xué yīnyuè hǎo shì hǎo, kěshì yǐhòu zhǎo gōngzuò bú tài róngyì.
examples.translation:
A: I plan to study music.
B: While it is good to study music, it may not be easy to find a job later.

examples.hanzi:
A: 这件衣服太贵了, 别买!
B: 这件衣服贵是贵, 可是牌子好。
examples.pinyin:
A: Zhè jiàn yīfu tài guì le, bié mǎi!
B: Zhè jiàn yīfu guì shì guì, kěshì páizi hǎo.
examples.translation:
A: This piece of clothing is too expensive. Don't buy it.
B: It is expensive, but it's a good brand.

examples.hanzi:
Q: 明天晚上是林雪梅的生日晚会, 你去吗?
A: 我去是去, 可是会晚一点儿。
examples.pinyin:
Q: Míngtiān wǎnshang shì Lín Xuěméi de shēngrì wǎnhuì, nǐ qù ma?
A: Wǒ qù shì qù, kěshì huì wǎn yìdiǎnr.
examples.translation:
Q: Lin Xuemei's birthday party is tomorrow night. Are you going?
A: I am going, but I'll be a little late.

usage_notes: This structure is used to express partial agreement.
The speaker first concurs with another person's statement before pointing out a different aspect that may have been ignored.
It can also be used to warn the other person of possible contingencies.

grammar_sections.title: …什么的
grammar_sections.explanation_en: …什么的 (...and so on) is used to cap a series of items.

examples.hanzi: 他要买毛衣、牛仔裤什么的。
examples.pinyin: Tā yào mǎi máoyī, niúzǎikù shénme de.
examples.translation: He wants to buy sweaters, jeans, and so on.

examples.hanzi: 妈妈昨天晚上请客，做了很多菜，像红烧牛肉、清蒸鱼、家常豆腐什么的，都很好吃。
examples.pinyin: Māma zuótiān wǎnshang qǐngkè, zuòle hěn duō cài, xiàng hóngshāo niúròu, qīngzhēng yú, jiācháng dòufu shénme de, dōu hěn hǎochī.
examples.translation: Mom invited people over last night. She made many dishes—beef braised in soy sauce, steamed fish, home-style tofu, and so on. All of them were delicious.

examples.hanzi: 我们这栋宿舍楼的家具不错，像床、桌子、书架什么的，都又好又新。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhè dòng sùshè lóu de jiājù bùcuò, xiàng chuáng, zhuōzi, shūjià shénme de, dōu yòu hǎo yòu xīn.
examples.translation: The furniture in our dorm is quite nice. The beds, desks, bookshelves, and so on are all good and new.

examples.hanzi: 他床上放了很多东西，有被子、毯子、衣服什么的。
examples.pinyin: Tā chuángshang fàngle hěn duō dōngxi, yǒu bèizi, tǎnzi, yīfu shénme de.
examples.translation: There's lots of stuff on his bed: a quilt, a blanket, clothes, and so on.

grammar_sections.title: 大小, 长短, 宽窄…
grammar_sections.explanation_en: Monosyllabic opposites such as 大 and 小; 长 and 短; and 宽 (kuān) and 窄 (zhǎi) are combined to form the abstract nouns 大小 (size), 长短 (length), and 宽窄 (width), respectively.

examples.hanzi: 你看看这一套，样子、大小、长短都合适。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ kànkan zhè yí tào, yàngzi, dàxiǎo, chángduǎn dōu héshì.
examples.translation: Take a look at this suit. The style, size, and length are all spot on.

examples.hanzi: 这条裤子长短正好。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo kùzi chángduǎn zhènghǎo.
examples.translation: The length of these pants is just right.

examples.hanzi: 你穿中号的，这套运动服也是中号的，大小正好合适。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ chuān zhōnghào de, zhè tào yùndòngfú yěshì zhōnghào de, dàxiǎo zhènghǎo héshì.
examples.translation: You wear a medium. This tracksuit is also a medium. The size is just right.

grammar_sections.title: 打折
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 打折 (to discount, to sell at a discount) means “on sale.” When coupled with numbers, the phrase indicates a specific percentage off: e.g., (打) 八折 is twenty percent off, (打) 七五折 is twenty-five percent off, and (打) 五折 or (打) 对折 is fifty percent off.

patterns.meaning_en: To give a certain percentage discount. The number indicates the final percentage of the original price to be paid (e.g., 八折 means 80% of the original price).

examples.hanzi: 你看看这一套，样子、大小、长短都合适，而且打八折。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ kànkan zhè yí tào, yàngzi, dàxiǎo, chángduǎn dōu héshì, érqiě dǎ bā zhé.
examples.translation: Take a look at this suit. The style, size, and length are all spot on, and it's 20% off.

examples.hanzi: 这个周末很多东西都打折，我们去买吧。
examples.pinyin: Zhège zhōumò hěn duō dōngxi dōu dǎzhé, wǒmen qù mǎi ba.
examples.translation: So much stuff is on sale this weekend. Let's go shopping.

examples.hanzi: 这个书架上个月八十块，现在打七折，是五十六块钱。
examples.pinyin: Zhège shūjià shàng ge yuè bāshí kuài, xiànzài dǎ qī zhé, shì wǔshíliù kuài qián.
examples.translation: This bookshelf was eighty dollars last month. Now it is thirty percent off. It's fifty-six dollars.

examples.hanzi: 我买了一张飞机票，打五折，一百二十五块钱。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ mǎi le yì zhāng fēijīpiào, dǎ wǔ zhé, yìbǎi èrshíwǔ kuài qián.
examples.translation: I bought a plane ticket at half price for one hundred and twenty-five dollars.

usage_notes: The number after '打' and before '折' indicates the percentage of the original price one has to pay. For example, '打八折' means paying 80% of the original price, which is a 20% discount.
打七五折 means paying 75% of the original price (a 25% discount).
打五折 or 打对折 means paying 50% of the original price (a 50% discount).

grammar_sections.title: (要)不然
grammar_sections.explanation_en: (要)不然 means “otherwise.” It is used to express a dire consequence if the advice or course of action prescribed in the previous clause isn't taken.

examples.hanzi: 这儿的冬天冷得很，你得再买一条毯子，要不然容易感冒。
examples.pinyin: Zhè'r de dōngtiān lěng de hěn, nǐ děi zài mǎi yì tiáo tǎnzi, yàoburán róngyì gǎnmào.
examples.translation: The winter here is really cold. You've got to buy another blanket; otherwise, you'll catch cold easily.

examples.hanzi: 找房子不要找离马路太近的，要不然会很吵。
examples.pinyin: Zhǎo fángzi bú yào zhǎo lí mǎlù tài jìn de, yàoburán huì hěn chǎo.
examples.translation: When you are looking for a house, find a place that's not too close to the street. Otherwise it'll be too noisy.

examples.hanzi: 别吃那么多肉，多吃点青菜，要不然你会越来越胖。
examples.pinyin: Bié chī nàme duō ròu, duō chī diǎn qīngcài, yàoburán nǐ huì yuèláiyuè pàng.
examples.translation: Don't eat so much meat. Have more green leafy vegetables or you'll put on more and more weight.

grammar_sections.title: 只好
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 只好 denotes the nonexistence of other options.

examples.hanzi: 那个电影我想今天看, 可是今天的票卖完了, 只好明天看了。
examples.pinyin: Nàge diànyǐng wǒ xiǎng jīntiān kàn, kěshì jīntiān de piào mài wán le, zhǐhǎo míngtiān kàn le.
examples.translation: I wanted to watch that movie today, but the tickets were sold out. I can only watch it tomorrow.

examples.hanzi: 我们去一个中餐馆儿吃饭, 我想吃烤鸭, 服务员说不卖烤鸭, 我只好点别的菜了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen qù yí ge zhōngcānguǎnr chīfàn, wǒ xiǎng chī kǎoyā, fúwùyuán shuō bù mài kǎoyā, wǒ zhǐhǎo diǎn bié de cài le.
examples.translation: We went to eat at a Chinese restaurant. I wanted to order roast duck, but the waiter said they didn't sell it. I had to order something else.

examples.hanzi: 我的车让妹妹开走了, 我只好坐公共汽车去上班。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de chē ràng mèimei kāi zǒu le, wǒ zhǐhǎo zuò gōnggòng qìchē qù shàngbān.
examples.translation: My younger sister was driving my car. I had to go to work by bus.

examples.hanzi: 我们打算去海边玩, 可是一看外边下雨了, 只好不去了。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen dǎsuàn qù hǎibiān wán, kěshì yí kàn wàibian xià yǔ le, zhǐhǎo bú qù le.
examples.translation: We had planned to hang out by the seashore, but when we saw it was raining, we had to call it off.

grammar_sections.title: 非…不可
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 非…不可 can mean that if one doesn't do something, something negative is bound to happen. It can also show strong resolution and will.

examples.hanzi: 你再不起床，上课非晚了不可。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ zài bù qǐchuáng, shàngkè fēi wǎn le bù kě.
examples.translation: If you stay in bed any longer, you'll definitely be late for class.

examples.hanzi: 你得的这个病非打针不可，要不然会越来越重。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ dé de zhège bìng fēi dǎzhēn bù kě, yàobùrán huì yuèláiyuè zhòng.
examples.translation: For this illness, you have no choice but to get an injection. Otherwise, it will get more and more serious.

examples.hanzi: 张天明买衣服非买名牌的不可。
examples.pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng mǎi yīfu fēi mǎi míngpái de bù kě.
examples.translation: Zhang Tianming insists on buying only designer clothes.

examples.hanzi: 我明年回国以后非找一个钱多的工作不可，要不然就再上学。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ míngnián huíguó yǐhòu fēi zhǎo yí ge qián duō de gōngzuò bù kě, yàobùrán jiù zài shàngxué.
examples.translation: I'm determined to find a lucrative job after I return to my country next year. Otherwise, I'll go back to school.

examples.hanzi: 小林吃鱼非吃清蒸的不可，别的做法他不喜欢。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Lín chī yú fēi chī qīngzhēng de bù kě, bié de zuòfǎ tā bù xǐhuān.
examples.translation: When Little Lin eats fish, he insists on steamed fish. He doesn't care for fish cooked any other way.

usage_notes: 非…不可 can mean that if one doesn't do something, something negative is bound to happen.
非…不可 can also show strong resolution and will.

grammar_sections.title: 标准
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 标准 (biāozhǔn) can be used as a noun meaning 'criterion' or 'standard'. It can also be used as an adjective, either attributively before a noun or as a predicate.

examples.hanzi: 你认为一个好老师的标准是什么?
examples.pinyin: Nǐ rènwéi yí ge hǎo lǎoshī de biāozhǔn shì shénme?
examples.translation: What do you think are the criteria for a good teacher? [noun]

examples.hanzi: 这个大学收研究生有什么标准?
examples.pinyin: Zhè ge dàxué shōu yánjiūshēng yǒu shénme biāozhǔn?
examples.translation: What are the criteria for accepting graduate students at this university? [noun]

examples.hanzi: 他说的是标准的北京话。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuō de shì biāozhǔn de Běijīnghuà.
examples.translation: What he speaks is standard Beijing dialect. [attributive]

examples.hanzi: 我说的上海话不太标准。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ shuō de Shànghǎihuà bú tài biāozhǔn.
examples.translation: My Shanghainese is not very standard. [predicate]

usage_notes: 标准 can be used as a noun, as in examples (1) and (2).
标准 can also be used as an adjective—as an attributive, as in (3), or as a predicate, as in (4).

grammar_sections.title: 在乎
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 在乎 is a verb. More often than not, it occurs in negative sentences.

examples.hanzi: 别人说什么,我不在乎。
examples.pinyin: Biérén shuō shénme, wǒ bú zàihu.
examples.translation: I don't care what other people say.

examples.hanzi: 你的车不见了,你怎么一点都不在乎?
examples.pinyin: Nǐ de chē bú jiàn le, nǐ zěnme yìdiǎnr dōu bú zàihu?
examples.translation: Your car has disappeared. How can you not care at all?

examples.hanzi: 他很在乎别人怎么看他。
examples.pinyin: Tā hěn zàihu biérén zěnme kàn tā.
examples.translation: He cares a lot how other people see him.

usage_notes: 在乎 is a verb meaning 'to mind' or 'to care'.
It most often appears in negative sentences, such as 不在乎 (don't care) or 一点都不在乎 (don't care at all).

context_examples.py: Shì shénme páizi de?
context_examples.translation: What's the brand?

context_examples.cn: 价钱不贵。
context_examples.py: Jiàqian bù guì.
context_examples.translation: The price is not expensive.

context_examples.cn: 不过,是纯棉的。
context_examples.py: Bùguò, shì chúnmián de.
context_examples.translation: However, it is pure cotton.

context_examples.cn: 买东西,我只买名牌的,要不然就不买,因为名牌的衣服质量好。
context_examples.py: Mǎi dōngxi, wǒ zhǐ mǎi míngpái de, yàoburán jiù bù mǎi, yīnwèi míngpái de yīfu zhìliàng hǎo.
context_examples.translation: When I shop, I only buy name brands, otherwise I don't buy at all, because name-brand clothes are better quality.

context_examples.cn: 你真时髦!穿名牌!
context_examples.py: Nǐ zhēn shímáo! Chuān míngpái!
context_examples.translation: You're so fashionable! Wearing name brands!

context_examples.cn: 你真时髦!穿名牌!那件好像是名牌的……哎呀,太贵了!
context_examples.py: nǐ zhēn shímáo chuān míngpái nà jiàn hǎoxiàng shì míngpái de āiyā tài guì le
context_examples.translation: You're so fashionable! Wearing name brands! That one looks like a name brand... Oh my, it's too expensive!

context_examples.cn: 因为名牌的衣服质量好。
context_examples.py: yīn wéi míngpái de yīfu zhìliàng hǎo.
context_examples.translation: because name-brand clothes are better quality.

context_examples.cn: 穿一、两次就不想穿了,只好再买一件。
context_examples.py: chuān yī, liǎng cì jiù bù xiǎng chuān le, zhǐhǎo zài mǎi yī jiàn.
context_examples.translation: After you wear them once or twice, you don't want to wear them anymore, so you have to buy another one.

context_examples.cn: 你说的有道理。
context_examples.py: Nǐ shuō de yǒu dàoli.
context_examples.translation: What you said makes sense.

standard_answers: have to, nothing but . . . would do

context_examples.cn: 买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好,但是也不一定非买名牌的不可。我买衣服的标准,第一是看穿着舒服不舒服,第二是质量好不好,第三是价钱合适不合适。是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。因为穿衣服是为了自己,不是为了给别人看。
context_examples.py: mǎi yīfu zhǐ kǎolǜ piányi dāngrán bù hǎo dànshì yě bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bùkě wǒ mǎi yīfu de biāozhǔn dìyī shì kàn chuān zhe shūfu bù shūfu dì'èr shì zhìliàng hǎo bù hǎo dìsān shì jiàqian héshì bù héshì shì shénme páizi de yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo wǒ dōu bù zàihu yīnwèi chuān yīfu shì wèile zìjǐ bù shì wèile gěi biérén kàn
context_examples.translation: Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands. My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they're comfortable to wear, second, whether the quality is good, and third, whether the price is suitable. I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not. Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.

context_examples.cn: 买衣服只考虑便宜当然不好,但是也不一定非买名牌的不可。我买衣服的标准,第一是看穿着舒服不舒服,第二是质量好不好,第三是价钱合适不合适。是什么牌子的,样子时髦不时髦,我都不在乎。因为穿衣服是为了自己,不是为了给别人看。
context_examples.py: mǎi yīfu zhǐ kǎolǜ piányi dāngrán bù hǎo dànshì yě bù yídìng fēi mǎi míngpái de bùkě wǒ mǎi yīfu de biāozhǔn dìyī shì kàn chuān zhe shūfu bù shūfu dì'èr shì zhìliàng hǎo bù hǎo dìsān shì jiàqian héshì bù héshì shì shénme páizi de yàngzi shímáo bù shímáo wǒ dōu bù zàihu yīnwèi chuān yīfu shì wèile zìjǐ bù shì wèile gěi biérén kàn
context_examples.translation: Of course it's not good to only consider price when buying clothes, but you don't necessarily have to buy name brands. My criteria for buying clothes are, first, whether they're comfortable to wear, second, whether the quality is good, and third, whether the price is suitable. I don't care what the brand is, or whether the style is fashionable or not. Because you wear clothes for yourself, not for others to see.

global_config.setting_summary_cn: 一个现代大学生的宿舍，以及一个宽敞明亮、人来人往的大型购物中心。
global_config.setting_summary_en: A modern college student's dorm room, and a spacious, bright, and bustling shopping mall.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A male Chinese college student in his early 20s, with a fashionable but slightly troubled expression. He is wearing casual clothes.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A friendly and helpful male Chinese college student in his early 20s, energetic and proactive.

character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student in her early 20s, cheerful and observant.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A female Chinese college student in her early 20s, stylish and opinionated.

hanzi: 张天明从家里来的时候,妈妈给他买了一些衣服,像T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤什么的,可是他觉得无论是样子还是颜色都不太好。

translation: When Zhang Tianming came from home, his mom had bought him some clothes, like T-shirts, sweaters, jeans, and so on, but he felt that neither the style nor the color was very good.

scenes.scene_goal: 展示张天明对妈妈买的衣服不满意，引出购物的动机。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 在张天明的宿舍里，他从一个行李箱里拿出几件衣服（T恤衫、毛衣、牛仔裤），皱着眉头看着它们，显得很苦恼。衣服的款式和颜色看起来有些过时。
scenes.setting_en: A typical college dorm room, slightly messy. A suitcase is open on the bed.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic medium shot, slow zoom in. A young Chinese man, Zhang Tianming, in his dorm room, looking frustrated. He holds up an unfashionable T-shirt and a dull-colored sweater from a suitcase on his bed. The clothes look like something a mom would buy. Natural light from a window.

scenes.camera_movement: Pan right to follow the group
scenes.video_prompt_en: Cinematic wide shot, panning right. Four young Chinese college students (two male, two female) are walking cheerfully towards the entrance of a huge, modern shopping mall. It's a bright, sunny Sunday. The mall has glass walls and prominent logos. Other shoppers are walking around.
